title: Java Optional Class
content:
### Optional Class

`Optional<T>` (Java 8+) is a container that may or may not contain a value. It helps avoid NullPointerException.

---

### Creating Optional

```java
Optional<String> empty = Optional.empty();
Optional<String> value = Optional.of("Hello");     // throws NPE if null
Optional<String> nullable = Optional.ofNullable(s); // accepts null
```

---

### Checking Value

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `isPresent()` | Returns true if value exists |
| `isEmpty()` (Java 11+) | Returns true if no value |
| `ifPresent(consumer)` | Execute if value exists |

---

### Getting Value

| Method | Returns |
|--------|---------|
| `get()` | Value or NoSuchElementException |
| `orElse(default)` | Value or default |
| `orElseGet(supplier)` | Value or supplier result |
| `orElseThrow(exceptionSupplier)` | Value or custom exception |
| `orElseThrow()` (Java 10+) | Value or NoSuchElementException |

---

### Transforming Optional

```java
Optional<String> opt = Optional.of("hello");

// Map
Optional<Integer> len = opt.map(String::length);

// Filter
Optional<String> longStr = opt.filter(s -> s.length() > 3);

// FlatMap
Optional<String> result = opt.flatMap(s -> Optional.of(s.toUpperCase()));
```

---

### Best Practices

| Do | Don't |
|----|-------|
| Use `orElse()` for defaults | Use `get()` without checking |
| Use `ifPresent()` for side effects | Use `Optional` for fields |
| Use `Optional` as return type | Use `Optional` in serializable classes |
| Use `Optional` in collections | Pass `Optional` as parameter |

> **Key Point:** Optional is primarily designed for return types to indicate that a value may be absent. It is not intended for use as a field type, method parameter, or in collections.

quiz.question: Which method returns a default value if Optional is empty?
